Plaintiff John Gengarella'S Motion To Stay Arbitration Proceedings
Off Calendar Feb-27-2019 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Susan Bishop, Esq. 408-286-5800 of Berliner Cohen for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Daniel Zamora, Esq. 415-772-9639 of Weintraub Tobin for Defendant OWSY LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, SCOTTISH AMERICAN CAPITAL.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Defendant does not contest the tentative ruling. Plaintiff argues procedural unconscionability and seeks clarification re: tentative ruling. Defendant argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Plaintiff John Gengarella'S Motion To Stay Arbitration Proceedings.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. For the reasons stated in the Court's tentative ruling granting Defendants' motion to compel arbitration and to stay arbitration proceedings, Plaintiff John Gengerella's motion to stay arbitration proceedings is denied.

Counsel for Respondents is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Defendants' Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation Pending Arbitration
Off Calendar Feb-27-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Susan Bishop, Esq. 408-286-5800 of Berliner Cohen for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Daniel Zamora, Esq. 415-772-9639 of Weintraub Tobin for Defendant OWSY LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, SCOTTISH AMERICAN CAPITAL.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views. Defendant does not contest the tentative ruling. Plaintiff argues procedural unconscionability and seeks clarification re: tentative ruling. Defendant argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT OWSY LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY, SCOTTISH AMERICAN CAPITAL'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Litigation Pending Arbitration.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants Owsy LLC and Scottish American Capital's motion to compel arbitration and stay litigation pending arbitration is granted. It is undisputed that Plaintiff John Gengerella's claims against Defendants in the Complaint for Damages and Declaratory Relief fall within the broad scope of the mandatory arbitration provision of the Employment Agreement between the parties. Although Scottish American Capital is not a party to the Employment Agreement, a nonsignatory defendant may invoke an arbitration clause to compel a signatory plaintiff to arbitrate his claims "when the causes of action against the nonsignatory are 'intimately founded in and intertwined' with the underlying contract obligations. [Citations.] By relying on contract terms in a claim against a nonsignatory defendant, . . . a plaintiff may be equitably estopped from repudiating the arbitration clause contained in that agreement." (Alliance Title Company, Inc. v. Boucher (2005) 127 Cal.App.4th 262, 271; see also, e.g., Garcia v. Pexco, LLC (2017) 11 Cal.App.5th 782, 787.) Those standards are met here, where Plaintiff alleges that Defendant Owsy is a subsidiary of Scottish American Capital, that Owsy was "fully dependent" upon Scottish American Capital, which has provided all funding for Owsy since its inception, and Plaintiff's charging allegations in the complaint frequently refer to Scottish American's alleged involvement in and responsibility for his termination.
